teks: Two AM. The team must ship. An AI deploy agent is running. Then Bedrock throttles — error four twenty nine. The agent stops. Deploy fails. An engineer wakes up for manual rollback. That is the problem DeadDrop solves.
DeadDrop — a deploy agent that survives when infrastructure fails. Infrastructure dies. Your agent doesn't. Built for the Resilient Agents hackathon on TrueFoundry and AWS Bedrock.

Four layers. One: TrueFoundry AI Gateway — when the primary model is full, we fall back automatically. Two: state machine with MySQL checkpoints. Three: MCP tools with circuit breakers and deploy to notifier degradation. Four: guardrails that block bad manifests before production.

This is not chaos theater. Every LLM call is written to provider log in MySQL. Scenario buttons only inject failures early — production uses the same gateway chain on real rate limits and timeouts. Guardrail hits are audited for SRE and compliance.

Live dashboard at deaddrop dot adindamochamad dot com. Scenario A normal — clean path, zero switches. Scenario B rate limit — Claude four twenty nine, circuit breaker opens, Mistral recovers, job done. Now D full chaos — watch the log: warnings, errors, guardrail block, deploy quarantine, notifier fallback. Metrics climb — switches, guardrails, recovery time. The job still finishes. Zero manual steps.

These counters are an audit trail for operators — not decoration. Jobs done, provider switches, tool failures handled, guardrail blocks, recovery milliseconds. TrueFoundry gateway, MCP, guardrails — one pipeline you can prove to a judge or an SRE.
DeadDrop — resilient deploy agent. TrueFoundry times AWS Bedrock. Try the live demo. Open source on GitHub. DeadDrop. Infrastructure dies. Your agent doesn't. Thank you.
